operating_state: P&G generated $82.0 billion in net sales with 2% growth driven by 9% higher pricing offset by 5% unfavorable foreign exchange and 3% volume decline. Organic sales grew 7% with mid-single-digit growth in Health Care and low single-digit increases across other categories except Grooming which declined. International markets contribute over 50% of revenue while Walmart represents 15% of total sales. Net earnings reached $14.7 billion with diluted EPS of $5.90 despite macroeconomic headwinds.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded 50 basis points to 47.9% as higher pricing and manufacturing productivity offset commodity costs, unfavorable mix, and foreign exchange impacts. SG&A increased 50 basis points to 25.7% as overhead costs rose from wage inflation despite productivity benefits. Cost pressures intensified from petroleum-derived materials, pulp, labor, transportation, and energy across global manufacturing operations.
material_events: The company returned $16.4 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases, marking the 67th consecutive year of dividend increases with quarterly dividend raised 3% to $0.9407 per share. Long-term debt increased to $24.4 billion including new EUR notes issuance. Operations in Russia were reduced with portfolio reductions and suspended activities beginning March 2022 due to the Russia-Ukraine war.
macro_exposures: Foreign exchange headwinds reduced net earnings by approximately $1.4 billion with significant impacts from weakening currencies in Argentina, Brazil, United Kingdom, Japan, Russia and Turkey. The Russia-Ukraine war negatively impacted operations with Ukraine representing less than 1% of sales and Russia approximately 2% where portfolio reductions occurred. Commodity cost inflation particularly affected oil-derived resins and paper-based materials while geopolitical tensions pose ongoing operational risks.
forward_outlook: Management maintains long-term growth algorithm targeting organic sales growth above market rates, mid-to-high single-digit Core EPS growth, and adjusted free cash flow productivity of 90% or greater. The company announced 2040 net zero emissions ambition with interim sustainability goals. Strategic focus remains on environmental sustainability, digital capabilities, supply chain optimization, and employee value equation while managing disciplined investment during macroeconomic pressures.
uncertainty_profile: Gillette indefinite-lived intangible asset valued at $14.1 billion shows 5% fair value cushion with sensitivity to discount rate or growth assumption changes. The company faces cybersecurity threats, tax law changes including potential U.S. corporate rate increases and global minimum tax implementation, and ongoing legal proceedings. Postretirement benefit obligations remain sensitive to discount rate assumptions with pension obligations partially offset by plan assets.
